bullets.text: US President Trump and Iran's negotiator exchanged warnings during talks in Switzerland, with Trump threatening to attack Iran if it does not restrain Hezbollah in Lebanon.
A global framework for reparatory justice was adopted at a conference in Ghana, including a call for formal apologies from countries that benefited from the transatlantic slave trade.
Colombians are voting in a runoff presidential election that is expected to lead to a shift in the country's decades-long armed conflict.
Authorities are warning Australians about the potential risks of using artificial intelligence to complete their tax returns.
Wesley Mission Queensland is closing its NDIS employment support program for young people with disability due to the "increasingly complex" NDIS environment.
A Melbourne woman lost over $646,000 in a romance scam, raising questions about whether banks and dating apps should be compelled to refund victims.
The latest data on the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge is anticipated to reinforce the consensus among US central bankers for further interest rate hikes this year.
Building materials group CRH is reportedly nearing its largest acquisition ever, with a deal to buy Dallas-based construction firm Arcosa, valued at almost $7 billion, potentially closing next week.
Hungary has appointed Gergely Tardos, head of research at OTP Bank Nyrt., as the new chief executive of the government's Debt Management Agency, with a mandate to implement cost cuts.
